title: Making Decisions: If 🛣️
description: Learn to execute code only if a condition is met.

slides.title: The Fork in the Road 🛣️
slides.content:
Up until now, our code runs straight down, line by line.

But real programs need to make decisions. 
- If the player has 0 health, show the Game Over screen.
- If a user entered the wrong password, deny access.

We do this using **If Statements**.

slides.title: The Syntax 📝
Seeing it work 👀
slides.content: Let's see an example using a literal boolean variable.

slides.title: When it's false ❌
slides.content: If we change the variable to `false`, the program completely ignores the `if` block and skips over it.

quizData.question: What happens if the condition inside an `if` statement's parentheses `()` is false?

options.text: It skips the code block inside the `{}`.
It runs the code inside anyway.

quizData.explanation: If statements are logical checks. If false, the instructions inside the block are skipped completely.
